nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; found at depths of 0–15 m in areas with rich coral growth

diet.notes: In the wild, has been documented feeding on sea anemones, polychaetes, and octocorallian and scleractinian coral polyps. In aquaria, has been known to accept chopped marine seafoods, mysid shrimp, brine shrimp, krill, and crustacean flesh. Generally advised to feed small amounts multiple times daily, particularly during the initial acclimation period.

summary: A vibrant Indo-Pacific butterflyfish with a yellow body and dark scale margins creating a distinctive lattice pattern. Generally considered not reef-safe due to a tendency to nip at corals and anemones; best suited to a large, peaceful fish-only system.
careNotes:
The Latticed Butterflyfish (Chaetodon rafflesii) is generally considered an intermediate-level saltwater fish. It tends to be shy and docile and has been reported to do well in pairs if both individuals are introduced simultaneously. Numerous hiding places among live rock are important for reducing stress, and a spacious tank with calm, peaceful tank mates is generally advised.

This species is commonly considered not reef-safe, as it has been documented preying on hard and soft corals, anemones, zoanthids, and tubeworms in the wild. Some sources suggest it may also occasionally nip at clams. It should not be housed with any corals or anemones.

A distinctive stress-related behavior has been observed: the fish may develop a dark blotch on its body when sleeping or stressed — this is a normal color change and typically resolves once the fish is well-acclimated and feeding regularly.

Water parameter cross-source summary: Temperature ranged from 22–29°C across sources (LiveAquaria 22–26°C, FishBase 25–29°C, TankFacts 24–27°C); the averaged range of 24–27°C is used here. SG ranged from 1.020–1.026 across sources; averaged min 1.022 and max 1.026. Tank minimum volume: LiveAquaria recommended 120 gal (454 L) and ReefApp 300 L; averaged to 378 L. Adult size: aquarium trade sources cite ~15 cm (6 in) as a common size, while FishBase records a scientific maximum TL of 18 cm — the range 12–18 cm is used here. No lifespan data was found across the sources consulted.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size, temperature range, native range, and taxonomy. FishBase max TL 18 cm; preferred temperature 25.1–29.3°C. Taxonomy: Order Acanthuriformes, Family Chaetodontidae.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (18 cm), minimum tank volume (300 L), salinity (~1.026), and temperament. Used in averaging disagreements on tank size and salinity.

additional.name: TankFacts
additional.notes: Additional cross-check on temperature (76–80°F = 24.4–26.7°C), pH (7.8–8.4), salinity (1.023–1.026), kH (8–12 dKH), and care level. Averaged into final parameter values.
